channel.title: Brennan Ryan Profile
channel.description: Recent content on Brennan Ryan Profile

item.title: Render Math With Mathjax
item.description:
Mathjax

Math equations can be rendered using Mathjax syntax with AMS symbol support.

Optionally enable this on a per-page basis by adding mathjax: true to your frontmatter.

Then, use $$ ... $$ on a line by itself to render a block equation:

$$ | Pr_{x \leftarrow P_{1}} [A(x) = 1] - Pr_{x \leftarrow P_{2}} [A(x) = 1] | < \text{negligible} $$

The raw version is:

$$ | Pr_{x \leftarrow P_{1}} [A(x) = 1] - Pr_{x \leftarrow P_{2}} [A(x) = 1] | < \text{negligible} $$

Write in-line equations with \\( ... \\) , like \( x^n / y \) . It’s easy!

item.title: Markdown Syntax
item.description:
Paragraph

Xerum, quo qui aut unt expliquam qui dolut labo. Aque venitatiusda cum, voluptionse latur sitiae dolessi aut parist aut dollo enim qui voluptate ma dolestendit peritin re plis aut quas inctum laceat est volestemque commosa as cus endigna tectur, offic to cor sequas etum rerum idem sintibus eiur? Quianimin porecus evelectur, cum que nis nust voloribus ratem aut omnimi, sitatur? Quiatem. Nam, omnis sum am facea corem alique molestrunt et eos evelece arcillit ut aut eos eos nus, sin conecerem erum fuga. Ri oditatquam, ad quibus unda veliamenimin cusam et facea ipsamus es exerum sitate dolores editium rerore eost, temped molorro ratiae volorro te reribus dolorer sperchicium faceata tiustia prat.

item.title: Emoji Support
item.description:
Emoji can be enabled in a Hugo project in a number of ways. :zap:

Emoji Support

The emojify function can be called directly in templates or Inline Shortcodes.

To enable emoji globally, set enableEmoji to true in your site’s configuration and then you can type emoji shorthand codes directly in content files; e.g.

The Emoji cheat sheet is a useful reference for emoji shorthand codes.

N.B. The above steps enable Unicode Standard emoji characters and sequences in Hugo, however the rendering of these glyphs depends on the browser and the platform. To style the emoji you can either use a third party emoji font or a font stack; e.g.

item.title: Placeholder Text
item.description:
Lorem est tota propiore conpellat pectoribus de pectora summo.

Redit teque digerit hominumque toris verebor lumina non cervice subde tollit usus habet Arctonque, furores quas nec ferunt. Quoque montibus nunc caluere tempus inhospita parcite confusaque translucet patri vestro qui optatis lumine cognoscere flos nubis! Fronde ipsamque patulos Dryopen deorum.

  1. Exierant elisi ambit vivere dedere
  2. Duce pollice
  3. Eris modo
  4. Spargitque ferrea quos palude

Rursus nulli murmur; hastile inridet ut ab gravi sententia! Nomine potitus silentia flumen, sustinet placuit petis in dilapsa erat sunt. Atria tractus malis.

item.title: Blog 1
item.description:
An Implication of Ethical Complication Aristotle’s Nicomachean Ethic states that moral behaviour is between 2 extremes:

  • Excess (using all available data, without ethical concern)
  • Deficiency (not using any data at all) Both of these extremes pose significant drawbacks, the former (excess) being unethical and risking any and all privacy of data. The latter (deficiency) being so cautious as to not gather data at all prevents any form of progress and innovation. This is where a concept known as the ethics equilibrium comes into play, a balance between the two extremes so as to foster growth while protecting the data privacy of everyone involved.

An Ethical Conundrum At the completion of a course, participants are given a survey of 10 questions. To perform the survey, participants access a URL in their web browser that is made available for a short period of time and requires that students log in with their username and password for the organization. The beginning of the survey prompts for the name of the course, but not their name. Instead, the survey description on this page indicates that all answers and the survey itself will remain anonymous. Executive management wishes to use the results of the survey to determine which courses require redesign or updating. You are the data analyst assigned to this task and are required to provide actionable insights for this purpose to executive management. Upon reading this you may be asking where the ethical concerns are? If at all? Here is my angle on the situation: Participants are required to login to complete the survey, while anonymity is promised, it may still be possible for executive management to determine the identity of each participant via the data provided by the participant logging in by cross-referencing the data available through the participant’s logins elsewhere on the same account. This would violate the promise of privacy safety and as well open the door to prejudice and bias where perhaps the executive management would prefer to take action depending on their preferred participants rather than working with anonymous data.
